summary: Added hard sanity gate for extremely irrational near-zero and near-one price entries. Gate blocks ask prices outside [0.05, 0.95] range — distinct from MIN/MAX_ENTRY_PRICE and NEAR_RESOLUTION gates.
gate_logic: Sanity gate (A2) injected early in check_entry_gates waterfall. Checks ask_side (computed from side: BUY_YES→ask_yes, BUY_NO→ask_no) against EXTREME_SANITY_FLOOR (0.05) and EXTREME_SANITY_CEILING (0.95). Rejects on either threshold breach with reason: 'extreme_sanity_floor:<price><threshold>' or 'extreme_sanity_ceiling:<price>><threshold>'.

env_vars.EXTREME_SANITY_FLOOR: 0.05 (default) — blocks ask prices below this threshold (near-zero irrational entries)
env_vars.EXTREME_SANITY_CEILING: 0.95 (default) — blocks ask prices above this threshold (near-one irrational entries)

rejection_reasons: extreme_sanity_floor:<ask_price><floor> — entry price too extreme (near zero)
gate_distinctness: Placed as A2 gate in waterfall, BEFORE spread, depth, imbalance, confidence checks. Distinct from: MIN_ENTRY_PRICE floor (0.22), MAX_ENTRY_PRICE ceiling (0.99), and NEAR_RESOLUTION_PRICE (0.97). Captures irrational extremes beyond normal operating range.
restart_note: Restart bot_core.py to enable gate. Gate activates on next check_entry_gates() call in entry loop. No database migration required.
next_steps: Deploy updated .env and core/risk.py to runtime
Monitor logs for extreme_sanity_floor/ceiling rejections to verify gate is working
